sections.paragraph:
ἀλόη, ης, ἡ (Plut., Mor. 141f; 693c; Diosc. 3, 22 et al.; PGM 7, 434; PLeid II, X, 12 and 36; PSI 1180,76; SSol 4:14 v.l. [αλωθ in text as rendering of אֲהָלוֹת cp. Ps 45:9; Pr 7:17 both Mt and here prob. product of the eaglewood or ‘Aqualaria agallocha’—ἀγάλλοχον or ‘A. mallacensis’]) aloes. In J 19:39 ἀλόη prob. refers to the strong aromatic, quick-drying juice of the ‘aloe vera’ or ‘aloe succotrina’, here mixed w. myrrh (ἀ. w. σμύρνα PGM 7, 434; SSol 4:14) and used for embalming (Ps.-Callisth. 3, 34, 4 ἀλόη and μύρρα [sic] are used to embalm Alexander’s corpse, but in his case a choice variety, such as Aquilaria ovata, would prob. have been used).—M’Clintock-Strong and ISBE, both s.v. ‘Aloes’, present options. S. also DELG.

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Diosc Diosc , or Dioscurides, I a.d. physician, also Ps.-Dioscorides after I a.d.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

PLeid PLeid = Papyri Graeci Musei Antiquarii Publici Lugduni-Batavi—List 4

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

prob. prob. = probable, probably

Ps.-Callisth Ps.-Callisth , Historia Alexandri Magni, II–III? a.d.—Lists 5

sic sic = so, thus (indicating an error or extraordinary form transcribed from the original)

ISBE ISBE = International Standard Bible Encyclopedia, rev. ed.—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 48
